findings.message: This claims `/track:full-cycle` conflicts with `track-transition`, but the documented flow already moves `todo` tasks to `in_progress` in `/track:implement` before the post-commit `done --commit-hash` step. The alleged direct `todo -> done` path is not part of the workflow, so this records a non-existent inconsistency.

The remediation hypothesis is stale: `apps/cli/src/commands/hook.rs::emit_verdict()` already prints blocked reasons to stdout on a block verdict, and `libs/domain/src/guard/policy.rs` already returns the specific policy messages. The observed `No stderr output` therefore is not caused by missing stdout emission in `policy.rs`.

WF-83 records the post-commit transition command as `cargo make track-transition T<n> done --commit-hash <hash>`, but the wrapper requires the track directory before the task id (`<track_dir> <task_id> <status>`). The implementation errors with the usage string when that first argument is missing, so this TODO entry preserves an invalid command shape for the workflow it is documenting.

FreeFunction validation semantics are inconsistent across the plan: IN-01 says forward checks must compare `expected_params`/`expected_returns` names, while AC-01 and T001 describe existence-check-only behavior, and T002 also contradicts itself by treating FreeFunction as both part of and outside the existence-only guard. The implementation rule needs to be made single-valued.

T004 points the Interactor codec update at `domain-types.json`, but `RenderContractMapInteractor` is declared in `usecase-types.json` (the surrounding S04 note already names the correct file). As written, this task sends the change to the wrong artifact.

T004 points the Interactor codec update at `domain-types.json`, but `RenderContractMapInteractor` lives in `usecase-types.json` and `plan.md` already describes the same step against the usecase catalogue. As written, the task directs the change to the wrong layer/file.

AC-04 says the L2 exemplar is `ValidationError (action=modify)`, but the catalogued `ValidationError` entry is still `action=reference` in `domain-types.json:156-160`, and no task in this plan changes that action. The declaration_only example therefore cannot be produced as specified.

T002 tells the implementer to include `free_function` in `EXISTENCE_ONLY_KINDS`, then immediately says `free_function is NOT existence-only`. Those two requirements conflict, so the stale-field guard behavior is ambiguous.

`IN-01` requires FreeFunction signal evaluation to validate both parameter and return-name matches, but `AC-01` and `T001` reduce the same check to existence-only behavior. The plan needs one rule here; as written, the FreeFunction forward-check semantics are inconsistent.

`T005` only applies `declaration_only` when a `modify` node with empty `expected_methods` is also absent from the edge set, but `IN-04`/`AC-04` define `declaration_only` solely by `action=modify` + empty methods, with `ValidationError` as the exemplar. This extra no-edge شرط will suppress the intended dashed border on referenced declaration-only nodes.

AC-09 is not achievable as written: T005/GO-05 define the L2 `declaration_only` category as edge-less by design, but this criterion requires the phase-2 dogfood to reduce isolated nodes from 9 to 0. The plan can style those nodes differently, but it does not eliminate their isolation, so the close-out target conflicts with the stated implementation.

GO-01 still says all 9 isolated nodes will be converted into nodes with edges, but IN-11/AC-09/T010 define the L1 nodes and ValidationError as resolved by dashed-border visual markers while remaining edge-less. The close-out target is therefore contradictory: implementers cannot both leave those nodes edge-less by design and satisfy GO-01's edge-for-all statement.

AC-09 says the dogfood result must be recorded in confirmation.md, while IN-11, T010, and the plan section all require verification.md. This gives the close-out task two different artifact names for the same evidence, so one of the acceptance checks will be missed or written to the wrong file.

The S05 section description defines declaration_only as action=modify + empty expected_methods + no edges, but T005 and AC-04 define it as action=modify + empty expected_methods regardless of edge presence. That extra edge-less condition changes behavior and can suppress the intended declaration_only styling for nodes that later gain field/return edges.

GO-01 requires converting all 9 isolated contract-map nodes into nodes with edges, but the same spec later defines the L1 nodes and ValidationError as resolved by dashed-border visual markers while remaining edge-less. That makes the close-out target contradictory: implementers cannot both leave those nodes edge-less by design and satisfy the 'edge for every isolated node' goal.

T001 says FreeFunction expected params/returns are declared for future renderer use, while the plan's L4 close-out relies on Phase 2 using those fields to produce FreeFunction edges. If T001 is followed literally, L4 cannot gain an edge in this track, so AC-08/T010 are not achievable as written.

The S05 section defines declaration_only as action=modify + empty expected_methods + no edges, but T005 in the same section says action=modify + empty expected_methods gets declaration_only regardless of edge presence. This gives two different rendering rules for the same classDef and can change behavior for declaration-only nodes that later gain field/return edges.

AC-09 requires recording dogfood evidence in confirmation.md, while IN-11, S10, and T010 all require verification.md for the same close-out result. This leaves the acceptance artifact ambiguous and can cause one acceptance path to be missed.

GO-01/GO-02/AC-09 require the L4 free-function case to be resolved by edge coverage, and domain-types.json says expected_params/expected_returns should generate method-call edges, but T001 explicitly leaves those fields for "future renderer use" and no later task implements FreeFunction param/return edge emission. Completing these tasks can therefore mark L4 resolved while only rendering a FreeFunction node, contradicting the stated Phase 2 edge-coverage goal.

IN-05/IN-08/AC-05 define field edges from catalogue entry expected_members / MemberDeclaration::Field, but T006 only adds domain storage/rendering and no task extends the catalogue codec/schema path to decode or encode expected_members from the JSON catalogues. Since the plan's integration tests and dogfood operate on fixture/catalogue entries, the field-edge acceptance criteria are not executable as written.

AC-08 requires layer-agnostic tests for a "free_function edge", but the same spec says L4 is only made renderable as a FreeFunction node in Phase 2 and that free-function edges are deferred to Phase 3; T009 likewise only asks for a FreeFunction kind node. As written, the acceptance criterion asks implementers to test behavior that this plan explicitly leaves out of scope, so it should say FreeFunction node/kind support rather than free_function edge.

T010 requires the dogfood run to show `render_contract_map` as a FreeFunction node and `ContractMapRenderOptions` with field edges, but the preceding tasks only add schema/renderer/codec support and fixture coverage. No task updates this track's own catalogue data from the temporary `value_object` placeholder to `free_function`, or populates `expected_members` for the entries that must produce field edges. Following the plan as written can therefore reach T010 without the data needed to satisfy AC-09.

CN-07 is mapped only to T004, but T004 is the Interactor codec extension and only covers `declares_application_service` encode/decode plus catalogue entry updates. It does not exercise or preserve the symlink/atomic-write constraint described by CN-07, so the task coverage claims this security constraint is handled by unrelated work.

The plan says Phase 2 dogfood will verify that all nine Phase 1 isolated nodes are resolved, including L4, but the same spec defines L4 as only rendering the FreeFunction node while deferring return/parameter edges to Phase 3. Rendering a FreeFunction node by itself does not create an edge to the isolated error type, so T010 cannot satisfy AC-09's L4 resolution claim unless this phase also implements FreeFunction param/return edge generation or narrows the acceptance criterion.

The `render_contract_map` catalogue entry omits `action`, which decodes as the default `add`, but AC-09/T010 rely on it being `action=reference` so the L4 free-function node receives `unused_reference` dashed-border treatment. T010 only says to change the kind to `free_function`, so the planned dogfood can complete without satisfying the stated L4 resolution.

T002's stale-field guard instruction is not executable as written: it says not to add `free_function` to `EXISTENCE_ONLY_KINDS`, but then says to add `FreeFunction` to `FORBIDDEN_FIELDS_EXISTENCE_ONLY` so params/returns are permitted. That list is for forbidden field names, not kind exceptions, so following the plan will not create a coherent codec rule for `expected_params` / `expected_returns`.

T002 says not to add `free_function` to the stale-field guard because serde's internally tagged enum will reject unexpected fields, but this codec path is explicitly guarded because `flatten` + internally tagged variants otherwise drop cross-kind fields. As written, the plan leaves `free_function` stale-field validation uncovered, so illegal catalogue fields can be silently accepted instead of rejected. Add an explicit guard/test for allowed `free_function` fields rather than relying on serde here.

IN-03 defines `unused_reference` as a reference node absent only from method / trait-impl edges, while AC-03 later requires absence from all edge types including Interactor-impl and field edges. If the implementation follows IN-03, a reference node that gains a field or Interactor impl edge can still be styled as unused, contradicting the acceptance criterion. Align IN-03 with AC-03's all-edge definition.

AC-09/T010 say the L4 close-out is satisfied by turning `render_contract_map` into a FreeFunction node, but the catalogue itself identifies `LoadAllCataloguesError` as the L4 isolated error type that lacked an edge source from the `load_all_catalogues` free function. No task adds a `load_all_catalogues` FreeFunction entry or otherwise connects that error type, so following the plan can still leave the documented L4 isolated node unresolved while the close-out claims all 9 Phase 1 isolated nodes are handled.

AC-06 requires `action_overlay=false` to produce Phase 1-identical output, but other Phase 2 requirements in the same spec (AC-03/AC-04 via T005) add always-on `unused_reference` and `declaration_only` classDefs/annotations to the rendered map. Once those fixes land, disabling only the action overlay cannot make the output Phase 1-identical, so the acceptance test/regression guard is infeasible unless it is scoped to action-overlay-specific output only.

AC-07 requires the `signals() == None` fallback to produce the same output as `signal_overlay=false`, but T008 instructs the implementation to add signal-specific classDef lines whenever `signal_overlay=true` and only says `signal_overlay=false` omits those lines. Implemented literally, a no-signal document would still differ from the disabled output, so the acceptance criterion and task plan disagree on the fallback behavior.

T010 claims the L4 fix is to add `load_all_catalogues` / `render_contract_map` as `free_function` entries with `expected_returns`, but then explicitly defers FreeFunction return-type edge rendering to Phase 3 and says both nodes have no outgoing edges. That contradicts GO-02/ADR L4, where the problem is the missing edge from a free function to its returned error type; under this plan `LoadAllCataloguesError` remains edge-isolated, so AC-09's claim that all 9 Phase 1 isolated nodes are resolved is not actually executable.

The infrastructure catalogue view reports `—` for the Signal column on every infrastructure entry, even though the track artifacts for this scope describe the infrastructure type signals as 4 Blue entries. This makes the generated plan view semantically inconsistent: readers see signal data as absent for the infrastructure layer while the signal overlay inputs are supposed to be present and Blue.

GO-01 now says Phase 2 only adds FreeFunction catalogue entries as preparation for Phase 3 return-type edge rendering, but GO-02 still says this track structurally resolves the L4 problem where return error types receive no edge. Since T001/T010 also defer expected_returns rendering to future use, the plan contradicts itself about whether Phase 2 actually fixes return-type edges or only prepares their sources.
